message:
fix(runtime): handle first resize event in coalescer without panicking

The resize coalescer used a let-chain pattern (if let Some(dt) = dt &&
dt >= threshold && ...) which implicitly skipped the first event ever
received (where dt is None), preventing immediate application even when
no debouncing is needed. Replace the let-chain with an explicit match
that treats None (first event, no prior timestamp) as time_ok=true,
allowing the first resize to apply immediately in Steady regime.

Also commit proptest regression file for animation tests to preserve
discovered shrunk counterexamples across CI runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc 6a2aa35acb4798df6a6012b021e951bc0eb27a049ff5f44982b8dcf75fb79116 # shrinks to stiffness = 10.0, damping = 85.69359570082786
